questions.questionText: Uncertainty in position of a minute particle of mass 25 g in space is 10<sup>-5</sup> m. What is the uncertainty in its velocity (in ms<sup>-1</sup>) (h = 6.6 $$\times$$ 10<sup>-34</sup> Js)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
According to Hysenberg's Uncertainty Principal $$$\Delta x . \Delta p \ge { h \over {4\pi}}$$$ Where $$\Delta x $$ is uncertainty in position and $$\Delta p $$ is uncertainty in momentum and as in momentum m (mass of object) is always same so we can say $$$\Delta x . m. \Delta v \ge { h \over {4\pi}}$$$ Mass of the particle (m) = 25 g = 0.025 kg

Position of the particle ($$\Delta x$$) = 10-5 m

$$\Delta x . \Delta p $$ = $${ h \over {4\pi}}$$

$$\Delta x . m . \Delta v $$ = $${ h \over {4\pi}}$$

$$\Delta v$$ = $${h \over {4 \times \Delta x \times m \times \pi }}$$

$$\Delta v$$ = $${{6.6 \times {{10}^{ - 34}}} \over {4 \times {{10}^{ - 5}} \times 0.025 \times 3.14}}$$

$$\Delta v$$ = 2.1 $$\times$$ 10-28
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: In a hydrogen atom, if energy of an electron in ground state is -13.6 eV, then that in the 2<sup>nd</sup> excited state is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
n = 1 means ground state

n = 2 means 1st excited state

n = 3 means 2nd excited state

Remember: nth excited state means n = n + 1

We know energy of an atom is given by

Energy (E) = -13.6 $$\times$$ $${{{Z^2}} \over {{n^2}}}$$

= -13.6 $$\times$$ $$1 \over 9$$ [ $$\because$$ Z = atomic number = 1 for hydrogen atom]

= -1.51 ev
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: The de Broglie wavelength of a tennis ball of mass 60 g moving with a velocity of 10 meters per second is approximately
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
As per de-Broglie wavelength of a particle is

Wavelength ($$\lambda $$) = $${h \over {mv}}$$

Where h is plank's constant, m is mass of the particle and v is the velocity of the particle

$$\therefore \lambda $$ = $${{6.63 \times {{10}^{ - 34}}} \over {0.06 \times 10}}$$

= $${{6.63 \times {{10}^{ - 34}} \times 10} \over {0.6 \times 10}}$$

= $${{6.63 \times {{10}^{ - 33}}} \over 6}$$

$$ \cong 10{}^{ - 33}$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Which one of the following groupings represents a collection of isoelectronic species? (At. nos. : Cs : 55, Br : 35)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Isoelectronic means those species whose electron number are same.

Species Atomic Number Electron Number
N3- 7 7 + 3 = 10
F- 9 9 + 1 = 10
Na+ 11 11 - 1 = 10
Be 4 4
Al3+ 13 13 - 3 = 10
Cl- 17 17 + 1 = 8
Ca2+ 20 20 - 2 = 18
Cs+ 55 55 - 1 = 54
Br 35 35
Mg2+ 12 12 -2 = 10

$$\therefore$$ N3-, F-, Na+
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: In Bohr series of lines of hydrogen spectrum, the third line from the red end corresponds to which one of the following inter-orbit jumps of the electron for Bohr orbits in an atom of hydrogen
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
In Bohr series of lines of hydrogen spectrum :- Red end means end of visible region which is balmer series of lines so n will be 2

Now for third lines with respect to Balmer series means n = (2 + 3) = 5th line

$$\therefore$$ n1 = 2 and n2 = 5

For 2 $$\to$$ 5 electron will need energy to jump as 2 > 5 so no spectrum will release instead energy will be absorbed.

$$\therefore$$ 5 $$\to$$ 2
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Which of the following sets of quantum numbers is correct for an electron in 4f orbital?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
For 4f orbital,

n = 4 and l = 3

Note : ( for s orbital l = 0, for p orbital l = 1, for d orbital l = 2, for f orbital l = 3)

Value of m = + l to - l

Here m = +3 to -3

Value of s = $$ + {1 \over 2}$$ or $$ - {1 \over 2}$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Consider the ground state of Cr atom (Z = 24). The number of electrons with the azimuthal quantum numbers, l = 1 and 2 are respectively
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Electronic configuration of Cr (Z = 24) =

1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p6 4s1 3d5

For 2p6 and 3p6 , l = 1. Here in 2p and 3p orbital total 6 + 6 = 12 electrons present.

For 3d5 , l = 2. Here in 3d orbital total 5 electrons present.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: The wavelength of the radiation emitted when in a hydrogen atom electron falls from infinity to stationary state 1, would be (Rydberg constant  = 1.097 $$\times$$ 10<sup>7</sup> m<sup>-1</sup>)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
We know Rydberg formula,

$${1 \over \lambda } = R \times {Z^2}\left( {{1 \over {n_1^2}} - {1 \over {n_2^2}}} \right)$$

[ for hydrogen atom Z = 1 ]

     = $$1.097 \times {10^7}\left( {{1 \over {{1^2}}} - {1 \over {{\infty ^2}}}} \right)$$

$$ \therefore $$ $$\lambda $$ = $${1 \over {1.097 \times {{10}^7}}}$$

= 9.11 $$ \times $$10-8 m

= 91.1 $$ \times $$10-9 m

= 91.1 nm

[ as 1 nm = 10-9 m ]
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Which one of the following sets of ions represents the collection of isoelectronic species? (Atomic nos. : F = 9, Cl = 17, Na = 11, Mg = 12, Al = 13, K = 19, Ca = 20, Sc = 21)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Isoelectronic means those species whose no of electrons are same.

Ions Atomic No No of Electron
K+ 19 19-1=18
Ca+2 20 20-2=18
Sc+3 21 21-3=18
Cl- 17 17+1=18


So, option $$(C)$$ is correct.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
In a multi-electron atom, which of the following orbitals described by the three quantum members will have the same energy in the absence of magnetic and electric fields?
(A) n = 1, l = 0, m = 0
(B) n = 2, l = 0, m = 0
(C) n = 2, l = 1, m = 1
(D) n = 3, l = 2, m = 1
(E) n = 3, l = 2, m = 0
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
As here is no electric and magnetic field so ignore m to calculate the energy of orbital.

As here atom is multi-electron so ( n + l ) rule is applicable here. This rule says those orbitals which have more value of ( n + l ) will have more energy.

In (A), n + l = 1 + 0 = 1

In (B), n + l = 2 + 0 = 2

In (C), n + l = 2 + 1 = 3

In (D), n + l = 3 + 2 = 5

In (E), n + l = 3 + 2 = 5

So (D) and (E) will be of equal energy.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Of the following sets which one does NOT contain isoelectronic species?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
In $$SO_3^{2 - }$$ no of electrons = 16 + 24 + 2 = 42

In $$CO_3^{2 - }$$ no of electrons = 6 + 24 + 2 = 32

In $$NO_3^{-}$$ no of electrons = 7 + 24 + 1 = 32

So they are not isoelectronic.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
Pick out the isoelectronic structure from the following :

$$\eqalign{ & \left( i \right)\,\,\,\,\,\,C{H_3}^ + \cr & \left( {ii} \right)\,\,\,\,{H_3}{O^ + } \cr & \left( {iii} \right)\,\,\,N{H_3} \cr & \left( {iv} \right)\,\,\,\,C{H_3}^ - \cr} $$
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Ions No. of electrons
CH3+ 8
H3O+ 10
NH3 10
CH3- 10


$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,$$ $${H_3}{O^ + },N{H_3}$$ and $$C{H_3}^ - $$ are isoelectronic.

questions.questionText: Uncertainty in the position of an electron (mass = 9.1 $$\times$$ 10<sup>-31</sup> kg) moving with a velocity 300 ms<sup>-1</sup>, accurate upto 0.001% will be (h = 6.63 $$\times$$ 10<sup>-34</sup> Js)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
% error in velocity = $${{\Delta V} \over V} \times 100$$

$$ \therefore $$ 0.001 = $${{\Delta V} \over {300}} \times 100$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\Delta $$V = 3 $$ \times $$ 10-3

According to Heisenberg uncertainty principle,

$$\Delta x.m\Delta V \ge {h \over {4\pi }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\Delta x = {h \over {4\pi m\Delta V}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\Delta x = {{6.63 \times {{10}^{ - 34}}} \over {4 \times 3.14 \times 9.1 \times {{10}^{ - 31}} \times 3 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}}}$$

             = 1.92 $$ \times $$ 10-2 m
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.
